content.content:
1	//! Standard I/O library — `io.*` functions and `file:*` methods.
2	//!
3	//! C source: `src/liolib.c` (841 lines, ~35 functions).
4	//!
5	//! PORT NOTE: This module necessarily requires file-system access. The PORTING.md
6	//! rule banning `std::fs` outside `lua-cli` conflicts with the crate assignment
7	//! (`lua-stdlib`). Every file-system call site carries a `TODO(port): std::fs`
8	//! marker. The architecture team must either relax the rule for this file, move
9	//! the module to `lua-cli`, or provide a thin IO-abstraction crate that wraps
10	//! `std::fs` under a permitted API.
11	//!
12	//! `popen` additionally requires `std::process::Command` and is stubbed.
13	//!
14	//! PORT NOTE: Rust's borrow checker prevents holding `&mut dyn LuaFileOps`
15	//! (extracted from userdata) and `&mut LuaState` simultaneously. The affected
16	//! functions (`io_read`, `f_read`, `io_write`, `f_write`, `io_flush`, `f_flush`,
17	//! `f_seek`, `f_setvbuf`, `get_io_file`) are marked with `TODO(port): borrow
18	//! split`. Phase B must restructure `g_read`/`g_write` to take a `StackIdx`
19	//! rather than a raw `&mut dyn LuaFileOps`, and use `RefCell` inside `LStream`
20	//! for interior mutability, or extract the file handle via a separate borrow
21	//! scope.
22	
23	use std::io::{self, SeekFrom};
24	
25	use lua_types::{LuaError, LuaType, LuaValue};
26	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
27	
28	// ── Constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	
30	/// Name of the file-handle metatable in the Lua registry. C: `LUA_FILEHANDLE`.
31	pub const LUA_FILE_HANDLE: &[u8] = b"FILE*";
32	
33	/// Registry key for the default input file. C: `IO_INPUT` = `"_IO_input"`.
34	const IO_INPUT_KEY: &[u8] = b"_IO_input";
35	
36	/// Registry key for the default output file. C: `IO_OUTPUT` = `"_IO_output"`.
37	const IO_OUTPUT_KEY: &[u8] = b"_IO_output";
38	
39	/// Number of bytes in the `"_IO_"` prefix, used to strip it in error messages.
40	/// C: `IOPREF_LEN`.
41	const IO_PREFIX_LEN: usize = 4;
42	
43	/// Maximum number of format-arguments passed to `file:lines`. C: `MAXARGLINE`.
44	const MAX_ARG_LINE: usize = 250;
45	
46	/// Maximum byte-length of a numeric literal read from a file. C: `L_MAXLENNUM`.
47	const L_MAX_LEN_NUM: usize = 200;
48	
49	/// End-of-file sentinel returned by `LuaFileOps::read_byte`. C: `EOF` == -1.
50	const EOF_SENTINEL: i32 = -1;
51	
52	/// Bulk-read chunk size, mirroring C's `LUAL_BUFFERSIZE`.
53	const LUAL_BUFFER_SIZE: usize = 8192;
54	
55	// ── Traits ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
56	
57	/// Capabilities required by the io library from an OS file handle.
58	///
59	/// TODO(port): Every concrete implementation of this trait requires either
60	/// `std::fs::File` (regular files), `std::io::Stdin`/`Stdout`/`Stderr`
61	/// (standard streams), or pipe handles from `std::process::Child` (popen). All
62	/// three are banned outside `lua-cli` by PORTING.md. This trait is the intended
63	/// abstraction seam for that restriction.
64	pub trait LuaFileOps: Send {
65	    /// Read one byte; return it as `i32`, or `EOF_SENTINEL` on EOF/error.
66	    fn read_byte(&mut self) -> i32;
67	
68	    /// Push back a previously-read byte for the next `read_byte` call.
69	    fn unread_byte(&mut self, byte: i32);
70	
71	    /// Write a byte slice; return the number of bytes actually written.
72	    fn write_bytes(&mut self, data: &[u8]) -> io::Result<usize>;
73	
74	    /// Flush any write buffers to the OS.
75	    fn flush(&mut self) -> io::Result<()>;
76	
77	    /// Seek within the file using a `SeekFrom` position.
78	    fn seek(&mut self, pos: SeekFrom) -> io::Result<u64>;
79	
80	    /// Return the current file position without moving it.
81	    fn tell(&mut self) -> io::Result<u64>;
82	
83	    /// Clear the error/EOF flag on the stream. C: `clearerr`.
84	    fn clear_error(&mut self);
85	
86	    /// Return `true` if the stream has a pending error. C: `ferror`.
87	    fn has_error(&self) -> bool;
88	
89	    /// Control stream buffering. C: `setvbuf`.
90	    fn set_buf_mode(&mut self, mode: BufMode, size: usize) -> io::Result<()>;
91	}
92	
93	// ── Enums ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
94	
95	/// Seek anchor for `file:seek`. C: `{SEEK_SET, SEEK_CUR, SEEK_END}`.

lines: +/// PORT NOTE: routes writes directly through `state.write_output()` rather than
+/// `g_write(getiofile(IO_OUTPUT))`. The full C path needs typed-userdata access
+/// to pull `LStream` out of the registry and an interior-mutability split to
+/// borrow its `LuaFileOps` alongside `&mut LuaState`; both land in Phase B.
+/// Until then, the only `IO_OUTPUT` ever installed by `luaopen_io` is `stdout`,
+/// so writing through `write_output` is behaviourally identical for the
+/// default case and lets `print`/`io.write` share the same sink.
pub fn io_write(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
-    // TODO(port): borrow split — extract file before calling g_write.
+    // C: int nargs = lua_gettop(L) - arg;  with arg == 1, so nargs == top().
+            // C: lua_isinteger ? fprintf("%lld", ival) : fprintf("%.14g", fval)
+                // gap as g_write — fine for now because integer tables hit the
+                // branch above and printf-style float formatting is unused on
+            // C: luaL_checklstring(L, arg, &l); fwrite(s, 1, l, f);
+    // C: returns the file handle (pushed by `getiofile`) so calls can chain
+    // as `io.write("x"):write("y")`. Mirror that by pushing the IO_OUTPUT
+    // userdata back onto the stack top.

content.text:
Build is green — no errors, only pre-existing warnings.

## Report

**Root cause.** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/io_lib.rs:991` — `io_write` was a Phase-B stub returning `LuaError::runtime("TODO(port): borrow split needed for io_write")`. The full C path is `g_write(L, getiofile(L, IO_OUTPUT), 1)`, but extracting `&mut dyn LuaFileOps` out of the registry-stored `LStream` userdata while keeping `&mut LuaState` live needs both typed-userdata access and `RefCell`-based borrow splitting — both are still Phase-B blockers documented in the trailer.

**Fix.** Replaced the stub with a direct implementation that mirrors `g_write`'s formatting (integer `"%lld"`, float `"%.14g"` placeholder, string-coerced bytes via `check_arg_string`) but writes through `state.write_output()` instead of a `LuaFileOps` borrow. The only `IO_OUTPUT` ever installed by `luaopen_io` is `stdout`, so this is behaviorally identical to the C path for the default sink — and routes `print`/`io.write` through the same writer. The function still returns the IO_OUTPUT userdata at the top so `io.write("x"):write("y")` chains. Documented as a `PORT NOTE` with the Phase-B caveat preserved. No temporary instrumentation was added or needs removal.

**New state.** Build passes (`cargo build -p lua-cli` clean). Did not re-run the program — per instructions, the mega-loop re-tests.

Build is green — no errors, only pre-existing warnings.

## Report

**Root cause.** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/io_lib.rs:991` — `io_write` was a Phase-B stub returning `LuaError::runtime("TODO(port): borrow split needed for io_write")`. The full C path is `g_write(L, getiofile(L, IO_OUTPUT), 1)`, but extracting `&mut dyn LuaFileOps` out of the registry-stored `LStream` userdata while keeping `&mut LuaState` live needs both typed-userdata access and `RefCell`-based borrow splitting — both are still Phase-B blockers documented in the trailer.

**Fix.** Replaced the stub with a direct implementation that mirrors `g_write`'s formatting (integer `"%lld"`, float `"%.14g"` placeholder, string-coerced bytes via `check_arg_string`) but writes through `state.write_output()` instead of a `LuaFileOps` borrow. The only `IO_OUTPUT` ever installed by `luaopen_io` is `stdout`, so this is behaviorally identical to the C path for the default sink — and routes `print`/`io.write` through the same writer. The function still returns the IO_OUTPUT userdata at the top so `io.write("x"):write("y")` chains. Documented as a `PORT NOTE` with the Phase-B caveat preserved. No temporary instrumentation was added or needs removal.

**New state.** Build passes (`cargo build -p lua-cli` clean). Did not re-run the program — per instructions, the mega-loop re-tests.
